methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $174M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Oxa
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Zeitview is a Series D-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Dan Burton that has raised $174M to date. The comparable universe in Drones & Autonomous consists of six private companies including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no public peers exist in this narrow segment.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $174M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D to subsequent-stage transitions, yielding $261M. A Peer-Median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data; the five same-sector peers lacked sufficient overlap in stage and valuation tier to produce reliable multiples.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $222M to $300M, with $261M as the median. This range is narrow in absolute terms but reflects the limited methodological foundation available. The primary uncertainty is the absence of granular peer valuation data at comparable stages; a more robust analysis would require additional same-stage or adjacent-stage transaction evidence in autonomous systems. The reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than market-based multiples introduces execution and market-timing risk into the estimate.
